guidances.note: FY26 standalone/consolidated turnover of INR1,157cr confirmed as 'at par with the guidance given in the beginning of the year' on the Q4 FY26 concall (27 May 2026).
Standalone sales planned at INR1,300-1,400cr for FY27 (~15-20% YoY growth vs FY26), covering ~40% R&D and ~60% production-area orders per management commentary.
New order booking of INR8,000-10,000cr and concurrent sales of ~INR7,500cr+ over the next 4 years, per Atim Kabra (Executive Director).

ARC (JV/subsidiary with Rafael) guided minimum 50% growth in both order booking and sales for FY27; visibility of INR200cr order book as of the call, sales expected to cross INR600cr (vs FY26 actual of INR360cr).

Board approved in-principle demerger of the Space, Meteorology and Hydrology business into a new entity (ASTPL) with 1:1 share entitlement to existing shareholders, subject to shareholder/regulatory/NCLT approvals.
Management said it was 'hopeful' a final radar product would be DRDO-certified within 6 months, in response to a direct analyst question — described as tentative, not a firm commitment.

Reiterated and re-timed: management now frames this as 'nearly triple' FY26's INR1,157cr turnover (~INR3,400-3,500cr) over a 4.5-5.5 year window from FY26, i.e. by FY30-31; separately floated a coming-decade ambition of '>USD 0.5bn revenue enterprise.' Value-cr uses the ~3x-turnover reading, not the 10-yr figure.
Timeline pushed out: management now expects DRDO to be ready to qualify the overall (Uttam AESA) radar only in FY28, versus the ~6-month hope expressed on the prior quarter's call.
